issues_found: H2 pilot (Chanin first-letter probe: vanilla=0.225, TopK=0.066, 70.9% reduction) and full-scale (Gini absorption: vanilla=0.015, TopK=0.068, 4.5x INCREASE) use incompatible absorption metrics. Every mitigation variant (JumpReLU, OrtSAE, ATM) has HIGHER absorption than vanilla in full-scale. Paper presents these without labeling which metric produced which numbers.

H5 paper invents a 5% threshold: '4.95% vs 5% threshold, not significant (p=0.42).' Actual pass criterion was >8% (computed from pilot AUCs). Achieved causal delta: 2.51%. This is a 5.49pp shortfall, not a 'marginal miss.'

Null controls use alpha=5 only while main H3 aggregates across [1,3,5,10,20]. At alpha=5: high-absorption=0.7485, low-absorption=0.7543, p=0.94 (statistically identical). Main H3 18.4% difference is driven by high-alpha (10, 20). Paper claims 'driven by high-alpha conditions' -- post-hoc explanation for an internal contradiction.

OrtSAE ablation compares without-penalty (0.230 at L0~920) against with-penalty (0.247 at L0~550) to conclude 'orthogonality penalty does not appear to reduce absorption.' Comparison is at unmatched L0 -- the very confound the paper criticizes. Self-contradictory ablation design.

Probe quality confound in CMI analysis: absorption rate correlates with probe F1 at rho=-0.67 (p<0.001). CMI-absorption analysis uses L0=82 where mean probe F1=0.817. Low-CMI letters may be harder to probe, causing both low CMI and artificially high absorption rates. Paper never computes partial correlation controlling for probe F1.

CMI-absorption correlation reverses sign across dimensions: d'=10: rho=-0.383, d'=20: rho=+0.048, d'=30: rho=+0.299, d'=50: rho=+0.197. Bonferroni-corrected p=0.236. Sign reversal is qualitative failure, not sensitivity issue. Paper cherry-picks d'=10 as primary dimension without pre-specification.

Entanglement hypothesis presented as named contribution without empirical support beyond H3 Spearman r=+0.35. 'Hub feature' analogy introduced without network centrality analysis. Abstract claims 'causally important features' -- steering sensitivity does not measure causal importance (would require ablation studies).

The n=4 sparsity-absorption correlation (r~+0.93) has two points at identical L0=50. Driven by only 3 unique L0 values. Presented as 'striking relationship' and one of four contributions. Bootstrap CI does not address sample-size limitation.

MCC~0.21 across ALL variants including Random control suggests Hungarian matching yields chance-level recovery regardless of training. Paper acknowledges MCC failure but does not address implication: absorption reduction may reflect sparsity-induced suppression rather than genuine hierarchical feature recovery.
Section 3.5 promises one-way ANOVA across all completed variants but this is not reported in Results. Unfulfilled promise undermines credibility.
